# Meeting Transcript Chunk Format

**For:** PostgreSQL / pgvector developer  
**Generated by:** `bubble/transcript_chunker.py` in the `web-change-tracker` pipeline  
**Last updated:** 2026-06-03

---

## Overview

When the pipeline detects a new NAIC meeting and finds a matching audio recording in S3, it:

1. Transcribes the recording via OpenAI Whisper (plain text, verbatim — no summarization)
2. Uses an LLM agent to segment the transcript by agenda item
3. Writes a JSONL file where **each line is one chunk**, enriched with NAIC classification metadata

These JSONL files are the input for vectorization and storage in PostgreSQL/pgvector.

### Important: transcript text is never modified

The `text` field in each chunk is a verbatim substring of the original transcript. The agent only decides where to split and which agenda item each segment belongs to — it does not paraphrase, summarize, or alter the text in any way. Concatenating all `text` fields in `chunk_index` order reconstructs the full original transcript.

---

## File Location

**Format:** JSONL (newline-delimited JSON), UTF-8  
**S3 path:** `s3://{artifacts-bucket}/transcripts/chunks/{RECORDING_STEM}.jsonl`  
**Example:** `transcripts/chunks/NAIC_LATF_2026-05-21.jsonl`

One file per meeting recording. Each line is a standalone JSON object (one chunk).  
Chunks are capped at ~800 words. A 2-hour meeting typically produces 30–80 chunks.

---

## Example Chunk

```json
{
  "text": "The chair opened discussion on the Risk-Based Capital Working Group update. The working group reported that the proposed changes to the C-2 factor tables have been reviewed by the subgroup and are ready for a comment period. Three comment letters were received and will be addressed at the fall meeting.",

  "chunk_index": 4,

  "agenda_item_title": "Risk-Based Capital Working Group Update",
  "agenda_item_status": "Existing",
  "agenda_item_official_title": "Risk-Based Capital (E) Working Group",
  "agenda_item_standardized_id": "E-RBC-WG",
  "agenda_item_official_id": "2026-LATF-04",
  "chronicle_topics": ["Risk-Based Capital", "Life Insurance", "Solvency"],

  "event_title": "Life Actuarial (A) Task Force",
  "event_start_date_time": "2026-05-21T09:00:00-04:00",
  "event_duration": "3h 0m",
  "event_url": "https://naic.org/meetings/latf-2026-05",

  "organization": ["NAIC", "Life Actuarial Task Force"],

  "alert_type": "New Meeting or Event",
  "alert_title": "LATF Spring 2026 Meeting — Agenda Posted",
  "alert_description": "The Life Actuarial Task Force posted its spring 2026 meeting agenda including RBC updates, mortality table revisions, and principle-based reserving items.",
  "alert_url": "https://naic.org/meetings/latf-spring-2026",

  "library_item_title": "Spring 2026 LATF Meeting Agenda",
  "library_item_url": "https://naic.org/documents/latf-agenda-2026-05.pdf"
}
```

---

## Field Reference

### Chunk fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `text` | string | **The chunk text — verbatim transcript segment. This is the field to embed/vectorize.** |
| `chunk_index` | integer | 0-based position within the file. Use this to reconstruct reading order. |

### Agenda item classification

The four parallel agenda item arrays from the structured alert are zipped together so each chunk knows exactly which NAIC agenda item it belongs to.

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `agenda_item_title` | string | Human-readable agenda item title (e.g. `"RBC Working Group Update"`). `"General Discussion"` for unattributed content (opening remarks, roll call, procedural discussion). |
| `agenda_item_status` | string | `"New"`, `"Existing"`, or `"N/A"` — whether this was a new vs. standing agenda item at this meeting. |
| `agenda_item_official_title` | string | Official NAIC committee/working group title. `"N/A"` if none. |
| `agenda_item_standardized_id` | string | Standardized NAIC identifier (e.g. `"E-RBC-WG"`). `"N/A"` if none. |
| `agenda_item_official_id` | string | Official meeting-specific agenda item ID. `"N/A"` if none. |
| `chronicle_topics` | string[] | Chronicle taxonomy tags for this agenda item (e.g. `["Risk-Based Capital", "Life Insurance"]`). Empty array if none. |

### Event / meeting context

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `event_title` | string | Name of the meeting/event (e.g. `"Life Actuarial (A) Task Force"`). |
| `event_start_date_time` | string | ISO 8601 Eastern Time. May be `"N/A"`. |
| `event_duration` | string | e.g. `"3h 0m"`. May be `"N/A"`. |
| `event_url` | string | URL to the meeting page. May be `"N/A"`. |

### Organization

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `organization` | string[] | Array of organization names (e.g. `["NAIC", "Life Actuarial Task Force"]`). |

### Alert context

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `alert_type` | string | Alert classification (e.g. `"New Meeting or Event"`, `"New or Updated Report or Other Resource"`). |
| `alert_title` | string | Short title of the detected change. |
| `alert_description` | string | Full description from the page-change agent. |
| `alert_url` | string | URL of the page where the change was detected. |

### Associated document (library item)

Populated if a document (agenda PDF, report) was associated with this meeting. Both fields are `null` if no document was found.

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `library_item_title` | string \| null | Document title (e.g. `"Spring 2026 LATF Meeting Agenda"`). |
| `library_item_url` | string \| null | URL to the document. |

---

## Notes for Implementation

- **Vectorize `text`** — this is the only field that gets embedded. All other fields are metadata.
- **`chronicle_topics` and `organization` are arrays** — store as `text[]` or `jsonb`.
- **Global unique key per chunk:** `(source file name, chunk_index)` — `chunk_index` is sequential within a file, not globally unique on its own.
- **`"N/A"` vs null:** String fields the agent couldn't populate come through as `"N/A"`. Fields the pipeline couldn't populate (library item fields when no document exists) come through as `null`.
- **`"General Discussion"` chunks:** Unattributed content — all `agenda_item_*` classification fields will be `"N/A"` except `agenda_item_title` which will be `"General Discussion"`.
- **One JSONL file = one meeting.** Filename mirrors the recording: `NAIC_LATF_2026-05-21.mp3` → `transcripts/chunks/NAIC_LATF_2026-05-21.jsonl`.
- **Files are idempotent** — the pipeline will not regenerate a chunk file if the S3 key already exists.
